OPPOSING MEMO OF P AND A IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS FILED BY PLAINTIFF CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EDGARDO CAMPOS' MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS IS GRANTED. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT PLEAD A REAL PROPERTY CLAIM WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 405.4. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY $2,140.00 IN FEES AND COSTS (8 HOURS AT $350 PER HOUR PLUS $40) PER CCP 405.38 BECAUSE THERE WAS NO SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION TO RECORD THE LIS PENDENS. GIVEN THE LACK OF COMPLEXITY OF THE MOTION, ANY MORE THAN EIGHT HOURS PROSECUTING THIS MOTION IS EXCESSIVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES' PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND REQUEST TO STAY PROCEEDINGS UNTIL COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT REINSTATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING FROM APRIL 19 AS FOLLOWS DUE TO DEFENDANTS' ACKNOWLEDGMENT THAT THEIR ARGUMENT BASED ON CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1281.5(B) LACKED MERIT. PLAINTIFF CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES' PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND REQUEST TO STAY PROCEEDINGS UNTIL COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE FILING OF THIS ACTION WAS NOT A WAIVER OR FORFEITURE OF THE RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. PARAGRAPH 19B(2)(IV) PERMITS THE FILING OF AN ACTION TO RECORD A LIS PENDENS. THE FAILURE TO REQUEST MEDIATION EARLIER IS NOT A WAIVER OR FORFEITURE OF THE RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. PARAGRAPH 19A PROVIDES THAT THE WAIVER OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS THE PENALTY FOR FAILING TO PURSUE MEDIATION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Notice Of Hearing On Motion/Pettiiton To Compel Arb And Request To Stay Procedings Until Completion Of Arbitration
Off Calendar May-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The court reinstates its tentative ruling from April 19 as follows due to defendants' acknowledgment that their argument based on Code of Civil Procedure 1281.5(b) lacked merit. PLAINTIFF CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES' Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request To Stay Proceedings Until Completion Of Arbitration is Granted. The filing of this action was not a waiver or forfeiture of the right to arbitrate. Paragraph 19B(2)(iv) permits the filing of an action to record a lis pendens. The failure to request mediation earlier is not a waiver or forfeiture of the right to arbitrate. Paragraph 19A provides that the waiver of attorney's fees is the penalty for failing to pursue mediation. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied . (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Hearing On Motion/Pettiiton To Compel Arb And Request To Stay Procedings Until Completion Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-19-2012 Continued To May-14-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Linnea N. Willis, Esq., of the Law Offices of Linnea N. Willis, Tel. 510.777.9795, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.
Nils Rosenquest, Esq., of Rosenquest & Associates, Tel. 415.292.0980, appearing for Defendant and opposing party.

Defendant's counsel submitted new authority to the court. The Court vacated its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES' Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request To Stay Procedings Until Completion Of Arbitration is continued to May 14, 2012. Supplemental opposition may be filed by 4/26/12 and limited only to the applicability of CCP 1281.5 Supplemental reply may be filed by 5/02/12. Both sides to provide courtesy copies to Department 302 by 5/07/12. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Oct-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LINNEA N. WILLIS APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. NILS ROSENQUEST OF ROSENQUEST & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 292-0980 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT EDGARDO CAMPOS;

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT EDGARDO CAMPOS' Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens is Granted. The complaint does not plead a real property claim within the meaning of CCP 405.4. Plaintiff shall pay $2,140.00 in fees and costs (8 hours at $350 per hour plus $40) per CCP 405.38 because there was no substantial justification to record the lis pendens. Given the lack of complexity of the motion, any more than eight hours prosecuting this motion is excessive. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)
